mnemonics: Child's Backpack: A child (component) carries a backpack (props) given to them by their parent to pass data along.
keyPoints: Props are the primary mechanism for passing data from a parent component down to a child component.
The child component receives the data as a read-only object in its props argument.
This creates a unidirectional data flow, making the application's behavior more predictable and easier to debug.
commonMistakes: Trying to mutate props directly within the child component (props are read-only).
Forgetting that props are passed down, not up; children cannot send data back to parents via props.
